page: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core operations, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's instruction set includes AVX-512 extensions, such as AVX512_VNNI and AVX512_BF16, and cryptography accelerations like AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to strong performance in extended instruction tests and single-threaded compression tasks. Multi-core performance is also strong, with Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core benchmarks ranking in the top 10% and top 25% respectively. Memory subsystem performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is in the top 25%, uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the middle 50%.

Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage or GPUs, the c8a.4xlarge offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory-to-CPU ratios. It is highly suited for single-threaded processing, image and text processing, and small-scale LLM inference, where it achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and general-purpose compute tasks, though it may face bottlenecks in memory-bound database operations or large-model LLM inference due to its average large-block memory bandwidth and 32 GB RAM limit.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but delivers 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside exceptional single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference speeds. However, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the c8a.4xlarge highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, including image and text processing, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference, while remaining less optimal for memory-heavy or large-scale database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 16-core AMD EPYC processor at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization for high-performance compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with a 16-core AMD EPYC CPU, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for intensive single- and multi-threaded processing.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and small-model LLM inference performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for compute-heavy tasks, image processing, and web serving
